annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58189,"title":"[Communication] Docs: Update README to document Unified Messaging APIs (ConversationAdministrationClient, ConversationThreadClie [Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages` **Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (#56911) by `@MaiLinhP` ## What Changed PR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. The migration brought in (or confirmed the presence of) two new hand-authored clients — `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` — as part of the Unified Messaging feature set first introduced in 1.3.0-beta.1. Custom suppression of `BuilderExtension` and `ClientSettings` was also added for types using `CommunicationTokenCredential` to preserve AOT compatibility. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` covers only the legacy WhatsApp-specific notification path (`NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`). The new **Unified Messaging** surface — `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` — is completely undocumented. - No examples show how to authenticate or instantiate `ConversationAdministrationClient` or `ConversationThreadClient`, including via `CommunicationTokenCredential` (whose `BuilderExtension`/`ClientSettings` were explicitly customised in this PR). - `ConversationThreadClientSettings` (new custom type added in this PR) is not mentioned anywhere in user-facing docs. - The `## Key concepts` section is a collapsed sub-heading inside `## Getting started` rather than a standalone `## Key concepts` H2 section, and its content is a single line that does not describe the two new clients. - The CHANGELOG `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is empty; the generator migration and new client-settings type should be acknowledged there. - No sample test file (`tests/Samples/`) exists for the Unified Messaging APIs (conversations), so there are no snippet sources for the README. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md` Add entries under the `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` header: ```markdown ## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Migrated library to new TypeSpec-based emitter. - Added `ConversationThreadClientSettings` to support configuring `ConversationThreadClient` with `CommunicationTokenCredential`. ``` #### 1b. Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` Restructure the README to comply with the standard template. Key changes: 1. Move `### Key concepts` out of `## Getting started` and make it a top-level `## Key concepts` H2 section. 2. Expand **Key concepts** to describe all four clients: `NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`, `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`. 3. Add a new **Authenticate with CommunicationTokenCredential** sub-section showing how to create a `ConversationThreadClient` with a token credential. 4. Add new **Examples** sub-sections for Unified Messaging: creating a conversation, listing conversations, sending/receiving messages on a conversation thread. Suggested **Key concepts** section content: ```markdown ## Key concepts - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends outbound notification messages (WhatsApp, SMS). - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves approved message templates. - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (Unified Messaging). - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (Unified Messaging). ``` Suggested **Authenticate the client** addition (append after the existing connection-string example): ```markdown #### CommunicationTokenCredential `ConversationThreadClient` also supports authentication via `CommunicationTokenCredential`: ``` var endpoint = new Uri(""); var tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); var settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings("", endpoint, tokenCredential); ConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationMessaging.cs` The file should: - Include `[Test]` (NUnit) methods, one per snippet. - Wrap snippet code with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. Snippets to implement: **Snippet: `CreateConversationAdministrationClient`** ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient var connectionString = ""; ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString); #endregion ``` **Snippet: `CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken`** (referenced in README above) ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken var endpoint = new Uri(""); var tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); var settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings("", endpoint, tokenCredential); ConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings); #endregion ``` **Snippet: `ListConversations`** ```csharp #region Snippet:ListConversations AsyncPageable conversations = adminClient.GetConversationsAsync(); await foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations) { Console.WriteLine($"Conversation id: {conversation.Id}"); } #endregion ``` Add README placeholders at the appropriate locations: ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections as top-level H2 (`##`) headings in this order: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but `Key concepts` is incorrectly nested inside it 2. ❌ **Key concepts** — must be moved to its own `##` section and expanded to cover all four clients 3. ✅ **Examples** — present but missing Unified Messaging examples 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — confirm the README is updated with the injected snippet bodies 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — regenerates API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24481510665/agentic_workflow) · ● 337.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58189","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-15T22:30:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T22:30:57Z"}

Add new **Examples** sub-sections for Unified Messaging: creating a conversation, listing conversations, sending/receiving messages on a conversation thread. Suggested **Key concepts** section content: ```markdown ## Key concepts - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends outbound notification messages (WhatsApp, SMS). - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves approved message templates. - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (Unified Messaging). - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (Unified Messaging). ``` Suggested **Authenticate the client** addition (append after the existing connection-string example): ```markdown #### CommunicationTokenCredential `ConversationThreadClient` also supports authentication via `CommunicationTokenCredential`: ``` var endpoint = new Uri(""); var tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); var settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings("", endpoint, tokenCredential); ConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationMessaging.cs` The file should: - Include `[Test]` (NUnit) methods, one per snippet. - Wrap snippet code with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. Snippets to implement: **Snippet: `CreateConversationAdministrationClient`** ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient var connectionString = ""; ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString); #endregion ``` **Snippet: `CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken`** (referenced in README above) ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken var endpoint = new Uri(""); var tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); var settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings("", endpoint, tokenCredential); ConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings); #endregion ``` **Snippet: `ListConversations`** ```csharp #region Snippet:ListConversations AsyncPageable conversations = adminClient.GetConversationsAsync(); await foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations) { Console.WriteLine($"Conversation id: {conversation.Id}"); } #endregion ``` Add README placeholders at the appropriate locations: ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections as top-level H2 (`##`) headings in this order: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but `Key concepts` is incorrectly nested inside it 2. ❌ **Key concepts** — must be moved to its own `##` section and expanded to cover all four clients 3. ✅ **Examples** — present but missing Unified Messaging examples 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — confirm the README is updated with the injected snippet bodies 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — regenerates API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24481510665/agentic_workflow) · ● 337.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58189","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-15T22:30:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T22:30:57Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue tracks documentation gaps in `Azure.Communication.Messages` following the TypeSpec emitter migration in PR #56911. The new Unified Messaging APIs (`ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`) are completely undocumented in the README, samples, and changelog.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages` (1.3.0-beta.3 unreleased)
**Affected API:** `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, `ConversationThreadClientSettings`
**Scenarios:**
- README only covers legacy WhatsApp notification path; Unified Messaging surface is entirely missing
- No authentication examples for `CommunicationTokenCredential` with the new clients
- `ConversationThreadClientSettings` (new custom type) not mentioned in any user-facing docs
- `## Key concepts` is incorrectly nested inside `## Getting started` instead of being a top-level section
- CHANGELOG `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is empty — TypeSpec migration and new types unacknowledged
- No sample test files exist for Unified Messaging APIs in `tests/Samples/`

**Root ask:** Update README, add samples, and populate the changelog to cover all new Unified Messaging APIs introduced in the TypeSpec migration.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue (no runtime bug). The gaps were identified by automated analysis of PR #56911 (commit `d2dee264`).

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` — confirm `Key concepts` is nested and Unified Messaging clients are absent.
2. Check `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md` — verify `1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` is empty.
3. Look under `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/` — confirm no sample file exists for Conversations.
4. Add samples file, update README structure and content, populate changelog as outlined in the Implementation Guide in the issue body.
5. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` after adding samples to inject snippet bodies into the README.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Communication.Messages` is a data-plane client SDK (not a management/ResourceManager package, not a Provisioning library).
- **Service:** `Communication` — The package lives under `sdk/communication/` and directly maps to the `Communication` service label, confirmed by reference issue #21270 which uses `Client` + `Communication`.
- **Confidence:** High — The issue explicitly names the package and service directory. No ambiguity between categories or services. Aligns with the quality reference issue pattern.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Communication` (line 214) — The issue's service label is "Communication", which matches this catch-all entry exactly.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @Azure/azure-sdk-write-communication
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners); no "needs-team-attention" added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Scanned CODEOWNERS bottom-to-top. Entries for `%Communication - Resource Manager %Mgmt` (line 948) require "Mgmt" — not present, skipped. Sub-service entries (`%Communication - Call Automation`, `%Communication - Chat`, etc.) require their specific service labels — not present, skipped. `%Communication` at line 214 was the first matching entry found.
</details>
